heading: Mandatory reporting for swine
label: 1635j Mandatory reporting for swine
notes: Amendments2015—Subsec. (c)(1)(D)(ii). , added cl. (ii) and struck out former cl. (ii). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The information published by the Secretary under clause (i) shall include a distribution of net prices in the range between and including the lowest net price and the highest net price reported. The publication shall include a delineation of the number of barrows and gilts at each reported price level or, at the option of the Secretary, the number of barrows and gilts within each of a series of reasonable price bands within the range of prices.”Pub. L. 114–54, § 102(b)(1)Subsec. (c)(3)(C). , added subpar. (C).Pub. L. 114–54, § 102(b)(2)2006—Subsec. (c). , amended heading and text of subsec. (c) generally. Prior to amendment, text related to daily reporting.Pub. L. 109–296, § 3Subsecs. (d), (e). , added subsec. (d) and redesignated former subsec. (d) as (e).Pub. L. 109–296, § 4
amendments: Amendments2015—Subsec. (c)(1)(D)(ii). , added cl. (ii) and struck out former cl. (ii). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The information published by the Secretary under clause (i) shall include a distribution of net prices in the range between and including the lowest net price and the highest net price reported. The publication shall include a delineation of the number of barrows and gilts at each reported price level or, at the option of the Secretary, the number of barrows and gilts within each of a series of reasonable price bands within the range of prices.”Pub. children.text: provide timely, accurate, and reliable market information;
facilitate more informed marketing decisions; and
promote competition in the swine slaughtering industry.

children.heading: General reporting provisions applicable to packers and the Secretary
children.label: b General reporting provisions applicable to packers and the Secretary

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish and implement a price reporting program in accordance with this section that includes the reporting and publication of information required under this section.

children.heading: Packer-owned swine
children.label: 2 Packer-owned swine
children.text: Information required under this section for packer-owned swine shall include quantity and carcass characteristics, but not price.

children.label: A
children.text: packer-sold swine; and

children.label: B
children.text: all other swine.

children.heading: Review
children.label: A Review
children.text: The Secretary shall review the information required to be reported by packers under this section at least once every 2 years.

children.label: i
children.text: information that is currently required no longer accurately reflects the methods by which swine are valued and priced by packers; or

children.label: ii
children.text: packers that slaughter a significant majority of the swine produced in the United States no longer use backfat or lean percentage factors as indicators of price.

children.label: i
children.text: is not generally available or maintained by packers; or

children.label: ii
children.text: would be otherwise unduly burdensome to provide.

children.heading: Daily reporting; barrows and gilts
children.label: c Daily reporting; barrows and gilts
i
children.text: comply with the publication requirements of this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provide for the timely access to the information by producers, packers, and other market participants.

children.heading: Reporting deadline and plants required to report
children.label: B Reporting deadline and plants required to report
i
children.text: not later than 7:00 a.m. Central Time on each reporting day, report information regarding all barrows and gilts purchased or priced, and

children.label: ii
children.text: not later than 9:00 a.m. Central Time on each reporting day, report information regarding all barrows and gilts slaughtered,

children.label: aa
children.text: barrows and gilts purchased; and

children.label: bb
children.text: barrows and gilts scheduled for delivery; and

children.label: II
children.text: the base price and purchase data for slaughtered barrows and gilts for which a price has been established;

children.label: aa
children.text: including any sum deducted from the price per hundredweight paid to a producer that reflects the repayment of a balance owed by the producer to the packer or the accumulation of a balance to later be repaid by the packer to the producer; and

children.label: bb
children.text: excluding any sum earlier paid to a producer that must later be repaid to the packer;

children.label: aa
children.text: the total amount paid for the barrows and gilts slaughtered at a packing plant during the applicable reporting period, including all premiums and discounts, and including any sum deducted from the price per hundredweight paid to a producer that reflects the repayment of a balance owed by the producer to the packer, or the accumulation of a balance to later be repaid by the packer to the producer, less all discounts; by

children.label: bb
children.text: the total carcass weight (in hundred pound increments) of the barrows and gilts;

children.label: III
children.text: information concerning the lowest net price, which shall be equal to the lowest net price paid for a single lot or a group of barrows or gilts slaughtered at a packing plant during the applicable reporting period per hundred pounds of carcass weight of barrows and gilts;

children.label: IV
children.text: information concerning the highest net price, which shall be equal to the highest net price paid for a single lot or group of barrows or gilts slaughtered at a packing plant during the applicable reporting period per hundred pounds of carcass weight of barrows and gilts;

children.label: aa
children.text: the total carcass weight of the barrows and gilts slaughtered at the packing plant during the applicable reporting period, by

children.label: bb
children.text: the number of the barrows and gilts described in item (aa),

children.label: VI
children.text: the average sort loss, which shall be equal to the average discount (in dollars per hundred pounds carcass weight) for barrows and gilts slaughtered during the applicable reporting period, resulting from the fact that the barrows and gilts did not fall within the individual packer’s established carcass weight or lot variation range;

children.label: VII
children.text: the average backfat, which shall be equal to the average of the backfat thickness (in inches) measured between the third and fourth from the last ribs, 7 centimeters from the carcass split (or adjusted from the individual packer’s measurement to that reference point using an adjustment made by the Secretary) of the barrows and gilts slaughtered during the applicable reporting period;

children.label: VIII
children.text: the average lean percentage, which shall be equal to the average percentage of the carcass weight comprised of lean meat for the barrows and gilts slaughtered during the applicable reporting period, except that when a packer is required to report the average lean percentage under this subclause, the packer shall make available to the Secretary the underlying data, applicable methodology and formulae, and supporting materials used to determine the average lean percentage, which the Secretary may convert to the carcass measurements or lean percentage of the barrows and gilts of the individual packer to correlate to a common percent lean measurement; and

children.label: IX
children.text: the total slaughter quantity, which shall be equal to the total number of barrows and gilts slaughtered during the applicable reporting period, including all types of purchases and barrows and gilts that qualify as packer-owned swine; and

children.label: iii
children.text: packer purchase commitments, which shall be equal to the number of barrows and gilts scheduled for delivery to a packer for slaughter for each of the next 14 calendar days.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of information regarding barrows and gilts purchased or priced, not later than 8:00 a.m. Central Time, and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of information regarding barrows and gilts slaughtered, not later than 10:00 a.m. Central Time,

children.label: I
children.text: a distribution of net prices in the range between and including the lowest net price and the highest net price reported;

children.label: II
children.text: a delineation of the number of barrows and gilts at each reported price level or, at the option of the Secretary, the number of barrows and gilts within each of a series of reasonable price bands within the range of prices; and

children.label: III
children.text: the total number and weighted average price of barrows and gilts purchased through negotiated purchases and negotiated formula purchases.

children.label: i
children.text: the packer’s best estimate of the total number of barrows and gilts, and barrows and gilts that qualify as packer-owned swine, expected to be purchased throughout the reporting day through each type of purchase;

children.label: ii
children.text: the total number of barrows and gilts, and barrows and gilts that qualify as packer-owned swine, purchased up to that time of the reporting day through each type of purchase;

children.label: iii
children.text: the base price paid for all base market hogs purchased up to that time of the reporting day through negotiated purchases; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the base price paid for all base market hogs purchased through each type of purchase other than negotiated purchase up to that time of the reporting day, unless such information is unavailable due to pricing that is determined on a delayed basis.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: B Publication
children.text: The Secretary shall publish the information obtained under this paragraph in the morning report as soon as practicable, but not later than 11:00 a.m. Central Time, on each reporting day.

children.label: i
children.text: the packer’s best estimate of the total number of barrows and gilts, and barrows and gilts that qualify as packer-owned swine, expected to be purchased throughout the reporting day through each type of purchase;

children.label: ii
children.text: the total number of barrows and gilts, and barrows and gilts that qualify as packer-owned swine, purchased up to that time of the reporting day through each type of purchase;

children.label: iii
children.text: the base price paid for all base market hogs purchased up to that time of the reporting day through negotiated purchases; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the base price paid for all base market hogs purchased up to that time of the reporting day through each type of purchase other than negotiated purchase, unless such information is unavailable due to pricing that is determined on a delayed basis.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: B Publication
children.text: The Secretary shall publish the information obtained under this paragraph in the afternoon report as soon as practicable, but not later than 3:00 p.m. Central Time, on each reporting day.

children.heading: Late in the day report information
children.label: C Late in the day report information
children.text: The Secretary shall include in the morning report and the afternoon report for the following day any information required to be reported under subparagraph (A) that is obtained after the time of the reporting day specified in that subparagraph.

children.heading: Daily reporting; sows and boars
children.label: d Daily reporting; sows and boars
A
children.text: comply with the publication requirements of this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide for the timely access to the information by producers, packers, and other market participants.

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: 2 Reporting
children.text: Not later than 9:30 a.m. Central Time, or such other time as the Secretary considers appropriate, on each reporting day, a packer required to report under paragraph (1) shall report information regarding all sows and boars purchased or priced during the prior business day of the packer.

children.label: A
children.text: the total number of sows purchased and the total number of boars purchased, each divided into at least three reasonable and meaningful weight classes specified by the Secretary;

children.label: B
children.text: the number of sows that qualify as packer-owned swine;

children.label: C
children.text: the number of boars that qualify as packer-owned swine;

children.label: D
children.text: the average price paid for all sows;

children.label: E
children.text: the average price paid for all boars;

children.label: F
children.text: the average price paid for sows in each weight class specified by the Secretary under subparagraph (A);

children.label: G
children.text: the average price paid for boars in each weight class specified by the Secretary under subparagraph (A);

children.label: H
children.text: the number of sows and the number of boars for which prices are determined, by each type of purchase;

children.label: I
children.text: the average prices for sows and the average prices for boars for which prices are determined, by each type of purchase; and

children.label: J
children.text: such other information as the Secretary considers appropriate to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Price calculations without packer-owned swine
children.label: 4 Price calculations without packer-owned swine
children.text: A packer shall omit the prices of sows and boars that qualify as packer-owned swine from all average price calculations, price range calculations, and reports required by this subsection.

children.heading: Reporting exception: public auction purchases
children.label: 5 Reporting exception: public auction purchases
children.text: The information required to be reported under this subsection shall not include purchases of sows or boars made by agents of the reporting packer at a public auction at which the title of the sows and boars is transferred directly from the producer to such packer.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: 6 Publication
children.text: The Secretary shall publish the information obtained under this paragraph in a prior day report not later than 11:00 a.m. Central Time on the reporting day on which the information is received from the packer.

children.heading: Electronic submission of information
children.label: 7 Electronic submission of information
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture shall provide for the electronic submission of any information required to be reported under this subsection through an Internet website or equivalent electronic means maintained by the Department of Agriculture.

children.heading: Weekly noncarcass merit premium report
children.label: e Weekly noncarcass merit premium report
A
children.text: each category of standard noncarcass merit premiums used by the packer in the prior slaughter week; and

children.label: B
children.text: the amount (in dollars per hundred pounds of carcass weight) paid to producers by the packer, by category.

children.heading: Premium list
children.label: 2 Premium list
children.text: A packer shall maintain and make available to a producer, on request, a current listing of the dollar values (per hundred pounds of carcass weight) of each noncarcass merit premium used by the packer during the current or the prior slaughter week.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 3 Availability
children.text: A packer shall not be required to pay a listed noncarcass merit premium to a producer that meets the requirements for the premium if the need for swine in a given category is filled at a particular point in time.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: 4 Publication
children.text: The Secretary shall publish the information obtained under this subsection as soon as practicable, but not later than 5:00 p.m. Central Time, on the first reporting day of each week.
